title: carmona microphylla
intro: Carmona microphylla, also known as the false chamomile or Mexican mint marigold, is a plant species in the mint family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Carmona microphylla include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating anxiety and stress, making it a popular natural remedy in many cultures. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support overall well-being.
